subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 5
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For gridded questions, enter just the number. Select all correct answers when asked.

questions.questionText: What multiplication fact does this array of dots show?
What is 9 × 6? Enter just the number.
What is 8 × 8? Enter just the number.
What is 72 ÷ 8?
There are 54 crayons shared equally among 6 boxes. How many crayons are in each box?
Which fact can help you solve 56 ÷ 7?
A garden has 8 rows of tomato plants with 4 plants in each row. How many tomato plants are there in all?
How many sides does this shape have?
Select all the expressions that have the same value as 6 × 9.
questions.explanation: 6 × 9 = 54. Six 9s added together equals 54, and 9 × 6 = 54. But 6 + 9 = 15 and 7 × 8 = 56, so those are not equal.

questions.questionText: A teacher arranges 48 chairs into 6 equal rows. How many chairs are in each row? Enter just the number.
A baker put 9 muffins on each of 6 trays. Then 5 more muffins were placed on a plate. How many muffins are there altogether?
There were 7 tables with 8 chairs at each table. Then 19 students sat down. How many chairs are still empty?
Each comic book costs $5. Priya buys 8 comic books and pays with a $50 bill. How much change does she get back?
Select all the expressions that are equal to 24.
Which number is shown by these base-ten blocks?
In the number 836, what is the value of the digit 8?
questions.explanation: The 8 is in the hundreds place, so its value is 800.

questions.questionText: What is 418 + 267? Enter just the number.
A school library had 604 books. Students borrowed 238 of them. How many books are still in the library?
Select all the numbers that round to 600 when rounded to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: 567 and 632 each round to 600. 471 rounds to 500 and 538 rounds to 500, so they are not correct.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 8 equal parts are shaded, so 3/8.

questions.questionText: What fraction of this bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 5 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, so 5/6.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 4 equal parts and the point is at the third mark, which is 3/4.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 3/4?
questions.explanation: 6/8 covers the same amount as 3/4, so they are equivalent.

questions.questionText: A bowl holds 8 pieces of fruit. 3 of them are pears. What fraction of the fruit are pears?
questions.explanation: 3 out of 8 pieces are pears, so 3/8.

questions.questionText: Which whole number is equal to the fraction 4/4?
questions.explanation: When all 4 of the 4 parts are shaded, the whole is filled, so 4/4 = 1.

questions.questionText: On a number line from 0 to 1, Point R is at 1/4 and Point S is at 1/3. Which statement is true?
options: Point R is farther from 0 than Point S
questions.explanation: With a numerator of 1, the smaller denominator makes the larger fraction. 1/3 > 1/4, so Point S is farther from 0 than Point R.

questions.questionText: Liam ate 3/6 of a granola bar and Mia ate 1/3 of a same-size granola bar. Who ate more, and why?
questions.explanation: 3/6 equals 1/2. Since 1/2 is greater than 1/3, Liam ate more.

questions.questionText: Select all the fractions that are equal to 1/2.
questions.explanation: 3/6, 4/8, and 2/4 each equal 1/2. 2/3 is greater than 1/2.

questions.questionText: Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: With the same denominator, fewer shaded parts means less: 2/6 < 5/6.

questions.questionText: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. A family eats 5 slices. How many slices are left? Enter just the number.
Which fraction is the smallest?
questions.explanation: When the top number is 1, the larger the bottom number, the smaller the fraction. 1/8 is the smallest because 8 is the largest denominator.

questions.questionText: What is the area, in square units, of this rectangle?
A rectangle is 9 centimeters long and 4 centimeters wide. What is its perimeter, in centimeters?
A rectangle has an area of 30 square feet. If one side is 5 feet long, how long is the other side, in feet? Enter just the number.
What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is just before 3 and the minute hand points to 10, which is 50 minutes, so 2:50.

questions.questionText: A movie started at 11:45 and ended at 12:20. How long was the movie?
questions.explanation: From 11:45 to 12:00 is 15 minutes, and 12:00 to 12:20 is 20 minutes: 15 + 20 = 35 minutes.

questions.questionText: A bucket holds 6 liters of water. How many liters of water are in 5 full buckets? Enter just the number.
The bar graph shows books read by each student. How many more books did Tessa read than Owen?
questions.explanation: Tessa read 10 and Owen read 4: 10 − 4 = 6 more books.

questions.questionText: Each ⚽ stands for 3 goals scored. How many goals did the Lions team score?
Select all the shapes that are quadrilaterals (shapes with exactly 4 sides).
questions.explanation: A trapezoid, rectangle, and square each have exactly 4 sides. A hexagon has 6 sides.
